title: $random
text: $random Generate a random string, raw data of a specified length, or a number between 0 and 1. $random("number") $random("string | raw ", Length) Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Topic String Data type of return values. Length Numeric Data length of return values which ranges from 1 to 128. Return Values $random returns three types of values. The type depends on the Topic. Table 2. Random values that can be returned by $random Topic Returned value String Printable string with a specified length. Raw Raw data with a specified length. Numeric Number between 0 and 1. If an error occurs, a negative value identifying the specific error is stored in $procerror , and an empty string ("") is returned. Table 3. Values commonly returned by $procerror following $random Value Error constant Meaning in context of $random -1110 UPROCERR_TOPIC Topic name not known. -1001 UPROCERR_STRING Generate random string failed. -1002 UPROCERR_NUMERIC Generate random number failed. -1007 UPROCERR_RAW Generate random raw data failed. -1203 UPROCERR_RANGE Value out of range. Length provided is not within the range 1 to 128. Use Allowed in all component types. Description The function $random generates a random string, raw data of a specified length, ranging from 1 to 128, or a random numeric value between 0 and 1. When generating a numeric value, the length argument is not used. If the topic is string or raw data, the length argument is required. Example: Using $random The following example shows how to use $random to generate a numeric, string, or raw data result and calculates its length: Explain this code variables numeric len, randomNum, strLen, byteLen string resStr raw resRaw endvariables len = 15 randomNum = $random("Number") ; randomNum = 0.73350636388324717805777460567361290307 strLen = $length(RandomNum) ; strLen= 40 resStr = $random("String", len); resStr = "usVf#G6"jj{kN4a" strLen = $length(resStr) ; strLen= 15 resRaw = $random("Raw", len) ; resRaw = "|i|3Np{]1Rz{O|Y+|${-|kr" byteLen = $length(resRaw) ; byteLen = 15, byte length, Setting $LENGTH_RAW_IN_BYTES = 1 strLen = $length(resRaw) ; strLen= 23, character length, Setting $LENGTH_RAW_IN_BYTES = 0 or not specified. Copy Related Links $encode Encrypt data, sign messages with digital signatures, or convert data from one encoding scheme to another. $decode Decrypt or decode data, or verify a message by means of a digital signature. numeric Uniface data type specifying a number to a maximum of 38 decimal places, including +, -, and decimal point . $length Return the length of the specified argument. $LENGTH_RAW_IN_BYTES Specify if function $length returns the length in bytes or characters for arguments with datatype Raw.
